methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $30M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 12 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Hybron operates in Robotics & Manufacturing at the Seed stage, having raised $30M to date. The valuation universe for robotics comprises 9 private peers—including 1X Technologies, Foundation Robotics, Divergent, Exotec, and Locus Robotics—and 1 public comparable (277810.KQ). However, the peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or narrow-tier matches to support median-based triangulation; only one adjacent-stage peer was identified, falling short of the minimum three-datapoint threshold.

Given this constraint, the Stage-Progression method is the primary applicable approach. Using the $30M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage progression, this method implies a $75M valuation. The 2.5x multiplier reflects observed patterns in frontier tech advancement from Seed through Series A funding environments.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $64M to $86M (±15% around the $75M median), derived by stress-testing the single-method result with sensitivity bounds. Key uncertainties include the absence of direct stage-matched peers, reliance on a historical step-up multiplier that may not reflect current robotics-sector financing conditions, and limited visibility into Hybron's unit economics or technical validation milestones. Additional fundraising or technical milestones would materially inform a subsequent round valuation.
